summarization.a: The figure shows the film thickness of various materials (TiN, Al₂O₃, SiO₂, Si₃N₄, HfO₂, ZrO₂) at 350°C using SiCl₄ as function of number of cycles
summarization.b: The figure compares the film thickness of Al₂O₃, HfO₂, and ZrO₂ with different atomic layer deposition (ALE) rates at 350°C using SiCl₄.

a.question: Does all the process use the same precursors (HF and SiCl4 combination) for etching?
Which materials get etched and which do not with HF anf SiCl4 combination?
a.answer: The film thicknesses after 50, 100, 200, and 400 ALE cycles using sequential HF and SiCl4 exposures at 350 °C. The ZrO2 and HfO2 films were etched linearly versus the number of ALE cycles. In contrast, there were no measurable thickness changes during 400 ALE cycles for the Al2O3, SiO2, Si3N4, and TiN films

b.question: What are the etch rates determined for the HF and SiCl4 reaction cycles?
b.answer:
Al₂O₃ ALE: 0 Å/cycle  
, HfO₂ ALE: 0.05 Å/cycle  
, ZrO₂ ALE: 0.14 Å/cycle

b.question: How is image b different from image a?
b.answer: Image b shows only those materials which get etched with HF anf SiCl4 from the group shown in image a
